VALUE ADDED TAX (AMENDMENT) BILL, 2026
VALUE ADDED TAX (AMENDMENT) BILL, 2026
A BILL FOR AN ACT TO AMEND THE VALUE ADDED TAX ACT
TO EXEMPT QUALIFYING UNPREPARED FOOD ITEMS AND
CERTAIN IMPORTED GOODS FROM VALUE ADDED TAX, AND FOR
CONNECTED PURPOSES
Enacted by the Parliament of The Bahamas
1. Short title and commencement.
(1) This Act, which amends the Value Added Tax Act (Ch. 370A), may be
cited as the Value Added Tax (Amendment) Act, 2026.
(2) This Act shall come into operation on the 1st day of April, 2026.
2. Amendment of section 6 of the principal Act.
Subsection (1) of section 6 of the principal Act is amended, in paragraph (b), by
the deletion of the words “if sold unprepared in a food store and”, immediately
after the words “Fourth Schedule”.
3. Amendment of Second Schedule to the principal Act.
The Second Schedule to the principal Act is amended —
(a) in Part I – Exempt Supplies, by the insertion, immediately after
item (18), of the following —
“(19) The wholesale and retail supply of goods referred to under
Part III if sold unprepared by a food store.”;
(b) in Part II – Exempt Imports of Goods, by the insertion, immediately
after item 9, of the following —
“10. The import of goods referred to under Part III by a food
store.”;
(c) by the insertion, immediately after Part II, of the following —
“Part III – Supply of Goods and Importations

VALUE ADDED TAX (AMENDMENT) BILL, 2026
OBJECTS AND REASONS
This Bill seeks to amend the Value Added Tax Act to eliminate VAT on qualifying
unprepared food items, thereby reducing the cost of essential goods for consumers and
on certain taxable imported goods.
Clause 1 of this Bill provides for the Short title and commencement provisions.
Clause 2 of this Bill amends section 6 of the principal Act by removing specific
wording related to unprepared food sold in food stores.
Clause 3 of this Bill amends the Second Schedule. Part I (Exempt Supplies) is
amended to include the wholesale and retail supply of unprepared goods sold by food
stores as exempt from VAT. Part II (Exempt Imports of Goods) is amended to exempt
the importation of unprepared goods by food stores from VAT. A new Part III is
inserted to specify the wholesale and retail taxable supplies and taxable importations
that qualify for VAT exemptions.
Clause 4 of this Bill amends the Fourth Schedule to align with broader
amendments to reduce the VAT on essential items which are imported or purchased by
consumers.
